claims.claim_text: Platform engineering curates common capabilities and experiences for internal users to reduce repeated work and cognitive load.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 3; section: 1. The Platform Exists To Reduce Repeated Complexity, paragraphs 1-3
claims.rationale: The CNCF Platforms White Paper explicitly describes platforms as user-defined capability collections that reduce common work and cognitive load for internal users.

claims.claim_text: DevOps is a methodology in which teams own the full path from application development to production operations, while platform engineering scales those principles through a shared platform.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Platform Engineering, DevOps, And SRE Are Related But Not The Same, paragraphs 1-2
claims.rationale: The CNCF Platforms glossary directly defines DevOps and explains platform engineering as a way to scale DevOps principles through a unified platform.

claims.claim_text: SRE commonly uses service level objectives and error budgets, and error budget status can inform release decisions.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Platform Engineering, DevOps, And SRE Are Related But Not The Same, paragraph 1; active check paragraph
claims.rationale: Google's SRE book directly supports the SLO and error-budget framing, including release-decision use, but `sre.google` is not on the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: An internal developer platform may include portals, APIs, CLIs, documentation, standards, and golden path templates; a portal is only one possible surface.
claims.span_hint: section: 3. Internal Developer Platforms Are Product Surfaces, Not Just Portals, paragraphs 1-2
claims.rationale: The CNCF Platforms glossary explicitly lists portals, APIs, CLIs, documentation, standards, and golden path templates as possible parts of a platform.

claims.claim_text: Platform self-service means users can request and receive capabilities autonomously and automatically with minimal manual intervention.
claims.span_hint: section: 5. Self-Service Must Include Guardrails, Not Just Automation, paragraphs 1-3
claims.rationale: The CNCF Platforms White Paper defines self-service as users being able to request and receive capabilities autonomously and automatically, on demand, with minimal manual intervention.

claims.claim_text: The platform maturity model treats custom manual request processes as lower maturity and self-service solutions, self-serve portals, and golden-path templates as higher-maturity platform interfaces.
claims.span_hint: section: 4. Golden Paths Are Opinionated Workflows With Escape Hatches; section: 8. Worked Example: Evaluating A Platform Decision
claims.rationale: The CNCF Platform Engineering Maturity Model explicitly contrasts manual custom processes with self-service solutions and names self-serve portals and golden-path templates as maturity signals.

claims.claim_text: Treating the platform as a product means investing in product management, user experience, feedback loops, and usage/value metrics rather than counting features shipped.
claims.span_hint: section: 6. Platform As A Product Means Adoption Is Earned, paragraphs 1-6; section: Did You Know, fact 4
claims.rationale: The maturity model's 'As product' investment and adoption sections explicitly call for product management, user experience, feedback loops, and data-driven indicators rather than feature-count output.

claims.claim_text: GitOps stores desired state in a versioned and immutable source, has software agents pull that state automatically, and continuously reconciles actual state toward desired state.
claims.span_hint: section: 7. GitOps And Infrastructure As Code Are Enablers, Not The Whole Platform, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: The upstream OpenGitOps principles file states the four defining properties: declarative, versioned and immutable, pulled automatically, and continuously reconciled.

claims.claim_text: Infrastructure as Code defines infrastructure with declarative configuration files instead of manual console or command-driven processes, making it versionable and repeatable.
claims.span_hint: section: 7. GitOps And Infrastructure As Code Are Enablers, Not The Whole Platform, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: HashiCorp's IaC primer directly defines infrastructure as code in terms of configuration files replacing manual GUI or command-line processes and emphasizes versioning and repeatability.

claims.claim_text: `kubectl apply --dry-run=client` only prints the object that would be sent and does not persist the resource.
claims.span_hint: section: 7. GitOps And Infrastructure As Code Are Enablers, Not The Whole Platform, paragraph before the `kubectl apply --dry-run=client` example
claims.rationale: The official `kubectl apply` reference explicitly says client dry-run prints the object that would be sent without sending or persisting it.

further_reading.title: CNCF CNPA Certification Page
further_reading.why_relevant: This is the canonical public overview of the CNPA exam context that this review module is preparing learners for.

further_reading.title: CNCF Platforms White Paper
further_reading.why_relevant: It is the primary CNCF reference for platform value, reduced cognitive load, self-service, and platform capabilities.

further_reading.title: Platform Engineering Maturity Model
further_reading.why_relevant: It gives the strongest primary framework for platform-as-product thinking, adoption, golden paths, and interface maturity.

further_reading.title: OpenGitOps Principles
further_reading.why_relevant: It is the concise upstream statement of the desired-state, pull, and reconciliation model behind GitOps.

further_reading.title: kubectl apply Reference
further_reading.why_relevant: It is the authoritative reference for the client dry-run behavior used in the module's hands-on validation example.

notes: Code blocks, Mermaid-like ASCII diagrams, quiz items, exercise steps, and most scenario tables were excluded unless nearby prose made a concrete, externally verifiable claim. The only off-allowlist claim I kept is the SRE/SLO/error-budget statement, which is marked `needs_allowlist_expansion` because the direct primary source is `sre.google`.
